aboutGame.title: What is Marvel Rivals Season 9?
paragraphs: Marvel Rivals is a free-to-play team-based hero shooter where players build Marvel squads, combine Team-Up skills, and fight across destructible multiverse battlefields. Season 9, The Mystery of Thebes, focuses on a major update cycle with new content, balance changes, and fresh meta shifts.

For a wiki site, the strongest content angle is not general game introduction, but practical Season 9 help: patch notes, hero picks, team-up explanations, map tips, beginner routes, and fast update summaries for players returning after a season break.

modules.name: Marvel Rivals Season 9 Patch Notes
modules.description: Break down the July 10 2026 Marvel Rivals Season 9 update with balance changes, Regenerative Shields, hero adjustments, and gameplay rhythm notes.

highlights.detail: Read the official Version 20260710 Balance Post to confirm hero-by-hero changes that land with Season 9.
Check the Regenerative Shields mechanic, which adjusts how Vanguards and Strategists manage incoming burst damage.
Review hero adjustments, including the Black Widow rework, before queuing ranked matches in the new season.
Compare buff and nerf directions to update your role priority, swap order, and counter-pick list for the new meta.
Track follow-up hotfixes and weekend patches on the official Marvel Rivals balance feed for late Season 9 tuning.

modules.name: Marvel Rivals Season 9 Beginner Guide
modules.description: Follow a practical starting route covering hero selection, role basics, team comps, objectives, and the early Marvel Rivals Season 9 meta.

highlights.detail: Pick a role that matches your playstyle, then learn the basic movement, cover usage, and ability cadence for that role.
Choose one easy-to-execute hero per role as your starting pool, focusing on heroes with clear team-up value.
Learn the objective flow on Convoy, Domination, and Convergence maps so you always know when to push, hold, or rotate.
Build team comps around one or two strong Team-Up pairings, then add flex picks that complement the pairing.
Review the Season 9 patch notes, role tier list, and counter-pick guide before climbing ranked in the new season.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.description: Quick answers to the most common questions about Marvel Rivals Season 9.

items.question: What is Marvel Rivals Season 9?
items.answer: Marvel Rivals Season 9 is The Mystery of Thebes, a major free-to-play update for NetEase's team-based hero shooter that adds new heroes, a new map, balance changes, and the new Regenerative Shields mechanic.

items.question: When does Marvel Rivals Season 9 start?
items.answer: Marvel Rivals Season 9 begins on July 10, 2026 UTC according to the official Dev Vision Vol. 19 announcement, with the Version 20260710 balance post landing on the same day.

items.question: Which Marvel Rivals Season 9 hero should beginners pick?
items.answer: Beginners in Marvel Rivals Season 9 should start with a Vanguard or Strategist that has a simple kit, clear team-up value, and forgiving cooldown windows, then expand the hero pool as the meta stabilizes.

items.question: How do Team-Up abilities work in Marvel Rivals Season 9?
items.answer: Team-Up abilities in Marvel Rivals Season 9 activate when specific hero pairings are on the same team, granting bonus effects that shape comps, counter-picks, and ultimate rotations.

items.question: What are Regenerative Shields in Marvel Rivals Season 9?
items.answer: Regenerative Shields are a new Marvel Rivals Season 9 mechanic that lets shields slowly recharge over time, changing how Vanguards and Strategists manage burst damage and sustained team fights.

items.question: How does the Marvel Rivals Season 9 Battle Pass work?
items.answer: The Marvel Rivals Season 9 Battle Pass runs alongside The Mystery of Thebes update and unlocks Mythic costumes, emotes, sprays, and currency as players progress through the season's free and premium tracks.

items.question: Which platforms support Marvel Rivals Season 9?
items.answer: Marvel Rivals Season 9 is available on Windows, PS5, PS4, Xbox Series X|S, the Epic Games Store, and the NetEase launcher, with crossplay enabled across all platforms.

privacyPolicy.title: Privacy Policy
privacyPolicy.description: Privacy Policy for Marvel Rivals Season 9 Wiki - how we handle visitor data and privacy on this fan-made guide hub.
content: This fan wiki provides informational game guides for Marvel Rivals Season 9. We do not request account credentials, Steam passwords, Discord credentials, or private payment information.
Basic analytics, advertising, and hosting providers may process standard technical information such as device type, browser, approximate region, and visited pages.
External links may lead to Steam, Discord, YouTube, X, Epic Games Store, PlayStation Store, or other Marvel Rivals community destinations. Those services are governed by their own privacy policies.

about.title: About
about.description: About Marvel Rivals Season 9 Wiki - an independent fan-built guide hub covering heroes, team-ups, maps, patch notes, and progression.
content: Marvel Rivals Season 9 Wiki is an independent fan-built guide hub covering heroes, team-ups, maps, patch notes, balance changes, official links, and beginner progression for The Mystery of Thebes update.

The site links players to official Marvel Rivals destinations including Steam, Discord, YouTube, X, Epic Games Store, PlayStation Store, and the official NetEase launcher while keeping fan guide content separate from official channels.

termsOfService.description: Terms of Service for Marvel Rivals Season 9 Wiki - usage terms for this independent fan-made guide hub.
content: This site is an independent fan-made guide hub. Content is provided for informational and entertainment purposes only.
Game systems, heroes, team-ups, maps, balance changes, and update details are covered as informational guide topics for Marvel Rivals Season 9 players.
By using this site, you agree not to misuse it, attempt unauthorized access, or present this fan wiki as an official NetEase Games, Marvel, Disney, or Marvel Rivals property.
